items.title: Egypt fume at 'injustice' and make Messi favouritism claims after exit
items.description: BBC Sport unpicks a game that will go down in World Cup history for featuring a team who were two goals down so late on, but who went on to win without needing extra time.
items.titleIt: Egypt fume at 'injustice' and make Messi favouritism claims after exit

items.title: Ambassador Tommasi Leaves Sudan: ‘Compromises Necessary for Lasting Peace’
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 04 LUG - Italy’s Ambassador to Sudan, Michele Tommasi, carried out his final mission in Port Sudan at the end of his four-year term. “I take with me a deep admiration for the dignity and resilience of the women and men of this country ,” he said during a farewell reception, “as well as the confidence that they will find the strength and wisdom necessary to reach the compromises essential for building lasting peace and a future of stability and prosperity.” The visit also provided a further opportunity to strengthen the bilateral partnership, with a particular focus on the he
items.titleIt: Ambassador Tommasi Leaves Sudan: ‘Compromises Necessary for Lasting Peace’

items.title: Humanitarian Corridors: 27 Refugees from Ethiopia Arrive in Italy
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 03 LUG - The process that enabled 27 Somali and Sudanese refugees from Ethiopia to depart for Italy—carried out through the “humanitarian corridors” program—has been successfully completed. The initiative, the result of a memorandum of understanding between the Community of Sant’Egidio, the Ministries of the Interior and of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation, and the Italian Episcopal Conference, represents a significant example of institutional cooperation and international solidarity. The embassy, which has been collaborating with the Community of Sant’Egidio on
items.titleIt: Humanitarian Corridors: 27 Refugees from Ethiopia Arrive in Italy

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenya: 'We Fear for Our Lives' - Kisumu Activists Explain Absence From Saba Saba Protests
items.description: [Capital FM] Kisumu -- For the first time in recent memory, Kisumu remained calm during the annual Saba Saba commemorations, with businesses operating normally, public transport running uninterrupted and traffic flowing freely across the lakeside city as organisers of planned protests cited security concerns for staying away from the streets.
items.titleIt: Kenya: 'We Fear for Our Lives' - Kisumu Activists Explain Absence From Saba Saba Protests

items.title: Russian FM Lavrov begins Africa tour with talks in Ethiopia
items.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov began his African tour with a visit to Ethiopia on Tuesday, where he met with Mahamoud Ali Youssouf, chairperson of the African Union Commission.
items.titleIt: Russian FM Lavrov begins Africa tour with talks in Ethiopia

items.title: Kenya: Orderly Protest Held in Mombasa After Police Agreement
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Residents in Mombasa were allowed to stage a 15-minute march protest through the city's Central Business District after consultations with police officers.
items.titleIt: Kenya: Orderly Protest Held in Mombasa After Police Agreement

items.title: OPEC+ boosts oil production as Gulf exports recover
items.description: Seven OPEC+ producers have agreed to increase oil production from August, signalling confidence that Gulf exports are recovering after months of disruption caused by the Middle East conflict. The move comes as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz gradually returns to normal.
items.titleIt: OPEC+ boosts oil production as Gulf exports recover

items.title: Conflict pushes South Sudan's healthcare system to breaking point
items.description: South Sudan's worsening conflict is placing enormous pressure on the country's healthcare system, with the International Committee of the Red Cross reporting a 50 percent rise in medical evacuations during the first half of 2026.
items.titleIt: Conflict pushes South Sudan's healthcare system to breaking point

items.title: Sudan: UN Condemns RSF Escalation Around El Obeid As Tasees Backs Ceasefire
items.description: [Dabanga] El Obeid / El Fasher / Geneva / Chad -- The UN Human Rights Council has unanimously condemned the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) for escalating violence in and around El Obeid, warning that the North Kordofan capital faces a growing risk of mass atrocities, while the RSF-aligned Sudan Founding Alliance (Tasees) called for an immediate ceasefire and renewed political negotiations.
items.titleIt: Sudan: UN Condemns RSF Escalation Around El Obeid As Tasees Backs Ceasefire
